- Packages: MSTest.TestFramework 3.8.0, MSTest.TestAdapter 3.8.0, Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0
- CI: Azure DevOps with `VSTest@3` task, TRX reporting, code coverage
- SDK: .NET 8

## Approach
Two valid approaches exist. Pick one:

- **Option A — Enable MTP via `EnableMSTestRunner` (minimal diff)**: Keep the existing
  `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` project format, add `EnableMSTestRunner=true` + `OutputType=Exe`
  centrally, and install MTP extension packages for TRX & coverage. Lowest risk; smallest diff.
- **Option B — Convert to MSTest.Sdk (recommended long-term)**: Switch each csproj to
  `<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/3.8.0">` which bundles MTP, TRX, coverage, and analyzers
  automatically. Cleaner csproj files; more upfront changes.

Both are documented below. Since you're on .NET 8 SDK (not .NET 10), you'll use `dotnet test`
in **VSTest bridge mode with `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true`** — MTP arguments go
after a `--` separator.

> **Critical**: Migrate all 5 projects together. Mixed VSTest + MTP in one solution is unsupported.

---

## Phase 0 — Prerequisites & Baseline

1. Record baseline test counts (pass/fail/skip) via `dotnet test`.
2. Ensure .NET 8 SDK ≥ 8.0.400 (`dotnet --version`).
3. Audit any `.runsettings` files for VSTest-only nodes.
4. Branch: `feature/migrate-to-mtp`.

---

## Phase 1 — Directory.Build.props (central configuration)

Add or update `Directory.Build.props` at the repo/solution root.

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- Adjust the condition to match your test-project naming convention -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <!-- .NET 8/9: bridge dotnet-test to MTP -->
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Show individual failures in dotnet test output -->
    <TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>true</TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

> **Naming convention**: If your projects don't all end in `.Tests`, adjust the
> `Condition` (e.g., `.Contains('.Test')`, `.EndsWith('Tests')`).
> Do NOT use `Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'"` — that property isn't
> defined yet when `Directory.Build.props` is evaluated.

---

## Phase 2 — Enable MTP Runner

### Option A — EnableMSTestRunner (minimal diff)

Add to the same `Directory.Build.props` condition block from Phase 1:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  <TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>true</TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Keep existing package references (`MSTest.TestFramework`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`,
`Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`) for now — they'll be cleaned up in Phase 6.

### Option B — MSTest.Sdk (recommended long-term)

**Step 2b-i.** Add MSTest.Sdk version to `global.json`:
```json
{
  "sdk": { "version": "8.0.400" },
  "msbuild-sdks": {
    "MSTest.Sdk": "3.8.0"
  }
}
```

**Step 2b-ii.** Convert each test `.csproj`:

```xml
<!-- BEFORE -->
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.8.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

<!-- AFTER -->
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/3.8.0">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Remove: `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`, `MSTest.TestFramework`,
`MSTest.Analyzers`, `IsPackable`, `IsTestProject`, `OutputType`, `EnableMSTestRunner`.

MSTest.Sdk's `Default` profile auto-includes TRX and CodeCoverage extensions —
skip Phase 3 if using Option B.

---

## Phase 3 — Install MTP Extension Packages (Option A only)

Since TRX reporting and code coverage were provided by VSTest infrastructure,
you need explicit MTP extension packages. Add to each test `.csproj`
(or via `Directory.Packages.props` if using Central Package Management):

```xml
<!-- TRX reports (replaces --logger trx) -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />

<!-- Code coverage (replaces --collect "Code Coverage") -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.0" />
```

---

## Phase 4 — Translate CLI Arguments

On .NET 8, MTP arguments must come **after a `--` separator**.

| VSTest argument | MTP equivalent |
|-----------------|----------------|
| `--logger trx` | `-- --report-trx` |
| `--collect "Code Coverage"` | `-- --coverage` |
| `--collect "Code Coverage;Format=cobertura"` | `-- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura` |
| `--filter <EXPR>` | `--filter <EXPR>` (same syntax — no change needed for MSTest) |
| `--results-directory <DIR>` | `--results-directory <DIR>` (same) |
| `--blame-crash` | `-- --crashdump` (+ add `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump`) |
| `--blame-hang --blame-hang-timeout 5m` | `-- --hangdump --hangdump-timeout 5m` (+ add HangDump NuGet) |

**Example combined command:**
```bash
dotnet test --no-build -c Release \
  -- --report-trx --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura \
     --results-directory ./TestResults
```

---

## Phase 5 — Update Azure DevOps Pipeline

### 5a. Replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`

```yaml
# ❌ BEFORE
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    runSettingsFile: 'test.runsettings'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true

# ✅ AFTER
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests (MTP)'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release --no-build
      -- --report-trx --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)
      --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
```

### 5b. Publish test results (TRX)

```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish TRX results'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/*.trx'
    mergeTestResults: true
```

### 5c. Publish code coverage

```yaml
- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish code coverage'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
```

### 5d. Handle zero-tests exit code (optional safety net)

MTP returns **exit code 8** when zero tests are discovered (VSTest silently succeeds).
If any project might legitimately discover zero tests, add `--ignore-exit-code 8`:

```yaml
arguments: >-
  ... -- --ignore-exit-code 8 --report-trx ...
```

---

## Phase 6 — Remove VSTest-Only Packages (after verification)

Once everything passes on MTP, these packages are no longer needed:

| Package | Why it can go |
|---------|---------------|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | Only needed for VSTest hosting; MTP doesn't use it |
| `MSTest.TestAdapter` | Only needed for VSTest discovery; `EnableMSTestRunner` replaces it |

Keep `MSTest.TestFramework` — that's your assertion & attribute API.

> If using Option B (MSTest.Sdk), these were already removed in Phase 2.

---

## Phase 7 — .runsettings Cleanup

If you have a `.runsettings` file, remove VSTest-only nodes:
`MaxCpuCount`, `ResultsDirectory`, `TargetFrameworkVersion`,
`TestAdaptersPaths`, `TestSessionTimeout`, data collector entries for
coverage (now handled by `--coverage` flag).

MSTest-specific `<MSTest>` settings inside runsettings are still supported.

---

## Phase 8 — Validation

1. `dotnet build` — zero errors.
2. `dotnet test` — all tests pass; counts match Phase 0 baseline.
3. Run a test exe directly: `./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyProject.Tests.exe` — confirms MTP is active.
4. Push to ADO — confirm TRX results and coverage appear on the pipeline summary.
5. Verify VS / VS Code Test Explorer discovers & runs tests (VS 17.14+).

---

## Checklist

- [ ] `Directory.Build.props` sets `EnableMSTestRunner`, `OutputType Exe`, `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`
- [ ] TRX extension package added (Option A) or MSTest.Sdk used (Option B)
- [ ] CodeCoverage extension package added (Option A) or MSTest.Sdk used (Option B)
- [ ] No `VSTest@3` tasks remain in pipeline YAML
- [ ] `DotNetCoreCLI@2` uses `--` separator before MTP args
- [ ] `PublishTestResults@2` and `PublishCodeCoverageResults@2` tasks added
- [ ] `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` and `MSTest.TestAdapter` removed (post-verification)
- [ ] Test counts match pre-migration baseline
- [ ] No `vstest.console.exe` invocations remain anywhere

---

## Risks & Rollback

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| TRX not generated | Ensure `--report-trx` is after `--` separator |
| Coverage % differs | Check `IncludeTestAssembly` setting (defaults to `false` in MTP) |
| Exit code 8 breaks CI | Add `--ignore-exit-code 8` |
| Rollback | Revert to `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` + old packages + `VSTest@3` |

---

## Future — .NET 10 SDK

When you upgrade to .NET 10 SDK, replace `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` with native
MTP mode in `global.json` and drop the `--` separator:

```json
{
  "test": { "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform" }
}
```

to global.json, remove `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, drop `--` separator.

# Migration Plan: VSTest → Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP)

## Current State
- 5 MSTest test projects using VSTest (NuGet-package setup)
- Packages: MSTest.TestFramework 3.8.0, MSTest.TestAdapter 3.8.0, Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk 17.13.0
- CI: Azure DevOps with `VSTest@3` task, TRX reporting, code coverage
- SDK: .NET 8

## Approach
Two valid approaches exist. Pick one:

- **Option A — Enable MTP via `EnableMSTestRunner` (minimal diff)**: Keep the existing
  `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` project format, add `EnableMSTestRunner=true` + `OutputType=Exe`
  centrally, and install MTP extension packages for TRX & coverage. Lowest risk; smallest diff.
- **Option B — Convert to MSTest.Sdk (recommended long-term)**: Switch each csproj to
  `<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/3.8.0">` which bundles MTP, TRX, coverage, and analyzers
  automatically. Cleaner csproj files; more upfront changes.

Both are documented below. Since you're on .NET 8 SDK (not .NET 10), you'll use `dotnet test`
in **VSTest bridge mode with `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport=true`** — MTP arguments go
after a `--` separator.

> **Critical**: Migrate all 5 projects together. Mixed VSTest + MTP in one solution is unsupported.

---

## Phase 0 — Prerequisites & Baseline

1. Record baseline test counts (pass/fail/skip) via `dotnet test`.
2. Ensure .NET 8 SDK ≥ 8.0.400 (`dotnet --version`).
3. Audit any `.runsettings` files for VSTest-only nodes.
4. Branch: `feature/migrate-to-mtp`.

---

## Phase 1 — Directory.Build.props (central configuration)

Add or update `Directory.Build.props` at the repo/solution root.

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- Adjust the condition to match your test-project naming convention -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <!-- .NET 8/9: bridge dotnet-test to MTP -->
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
    <!-- Show individual failures in dotnet test output -->
    <TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>true</TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

> **Naming convention**: If your projects don't all end in `.Tests`, adjust the
> `Condition` (e.g., `.Contains('.Test')`, `.EndsWith('Tests')`).
> Do NOT use `Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'"` — that property isn't
> defined yet when `Directory.Build.props` is evaluated.

---

## Phase 2 — Enable MTP Runner

### Option A — EnableMSTestRunner (minimal diff)

Add to the same `Directory.Build.props` condition block from Phase 1:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
  <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
  <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  <TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>true</TestingPlatformShowTestsFailure>
</PropertyGroup>
```

Keep existing package references (`MSTest.TestFramework`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`,
`Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`) for now — they'll be cleaned up in Phase 6.

### Option B — MSTest.Sdk (recommended long-term)

**Step 2b-i.** Add MSTest.Sdk version to `global.json`:
```json
{
  "sdk": { "version": "8.0.400" },
  "msbuild-sdks": {
    "MSTest.Sdk": "3.8.0"
  }
}
```

**Step 2b-ii.** Convert each test `.csproj`:

```xml
<!-- BEFORE -->
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.13.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestAdapter" Version="3.8.0" />
    <PackageReference Include="MSTest.TestFramework" Version="3.8.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

<!-- AFTER -->
<Project Sdk="MSTest.Sdk/3.8.0">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

Remove: `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, `MSTest.TestAdapter`, `MSTest.TestFramework`,
`MSTest.Analyzers`, `IsPackable`, `IsTestProject`, `OutputType`, `EnableMSTestRunner`.

MSTest.Sdk's `Default` profile auto-includes TRX and CodeCoverage extensions —
skip Phase 3 if using Option B.

---

## Phase 3 — Install MTP Extension Packages (Option A only)

Since TRX reporting and code coverage were provided by VSTest infrastructure,
you need explicit MTP extension packages. Add to each test `.csproj`
(or via `Directory.Packages.props` if using Central Package Management):

```xml
<!-- TRX reports (replaces --logger trx) -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.TrxReport" Version="1.6.2" />

<!-- Code coverage (replaces --collect "Code Coverage") -->
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.0" />
```

---

## Phase 4 — Translate CLI Arguments

On .NET 8, MTP arguments must come **after a `--` separator**.

| VSTest argument | MTP equivalent |
|-----------------|----------------|
| `--logger trx` | `-- --report-trx` |
| `--collect "Code Coverage"` | `-- --coverage` |
| `--collect "Code Coverage;Format=cobertura"` | `-- --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura` |
| `--filter <EXPR>` | `--filter <EXPR>` (same syntax — no change needed for MSTest) |
| `--results-directory <DIR>` | `--results-directory <DIR>` (same) |
| `--blame-crash` | `-- --crashdump` (+ add `Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CrashDump`) |
| `--blame-hang --blame-hang-timeout 5m` | `-- --hangdump --hangdump-timeout 5m` (+ add HangDump NuGet) |

**Example combined command:**
```bash
dotnet test --no-build -c Release \
  -- --report-trx --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura \
     --results-directory ./TestResults
```

---

## Phase 5 — Update Azure DevOps Pipeline

### 5a. Replace `VSTest@3` with `DotNetCoreCLI@2`

```yaml
# ❌ BEFORE
- task: VSTest@3
  inputs:
    testAssemblyVer2: '**/*Tests.dll'
    runSettingsFile: 'test.runsettings'
    codeCoverageEnabled: true

# ✅ AFTER
- task: DotNetCoreCLI@2
  displayName: 'Run Tests (MTP)'
  inputs:
    command: 'test'
    projects: '**/*Tests.csproj'
    arguments: >-
      --configuration Release --no-build
      -- --report-trx --results-directory $(Agent.TempDirectory)
      --coverage --coverage-output-format cobertura
```

### 5b. Publish test results (TRX)

```yaml
- task: PublishTestResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish TRX results'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    testResultsFormat: 'VSTest'
    testResultsFiles: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/*.trx'
    mergeTestResults: true
```

### 5c. Publish code coverage

```yaml
- task: PublishCodeCoverageResults@2
  displayName: 'Publish code coverage'
  condition: always()
  inputs:
    summaryFileLocation: '$(Agent.TempDirectory)/**/coverage.cobertura.xml'
```

### 5d. Handle zero-tests exit code (optional safety net)

MTP returns **exit code 8** when zero tests are discovered (VSTest silently succeeds).
If any project might legitimately discover zero tests, add `--ignore-exit-code 8`:

```yaml
arguments: >-
  ... -- --ignore-exit-code 8 --report-trx ...
```

---

## Phase 6 — Remove VSTest-Only Packages (after verification)

Once everything passes on MTP, these packages are no longer needed:

| Package | Why it can go |
|---------|---------------|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | Only needed for VSTest hosting; MTP doesn't use it |
| `MSTest.TestAdapter` | Only needed for VSTest discovery; `EnableMSTestRunner` replaces it |

Keep `MSTest.TestFramework` — that's your assertion & attribute API.

> If using Option B (MSTest.Sdk), these were already removed in Phase 2.

---

## Phase 7 — .runsettings Cleanup

If you have a `.runsettings` file, remove VSTest-only nodes:
`MaxCpuCount`, `ResultsDirectory`, `TargetFrameworkVersion`,
`TestAdaptersPaths`, `TestSessionTimeout`, data collector entries for
coverage (now handled by `--coverage` flag).

MSTest-specific `<MSTest>` settings inside runsettings are still supported.

---

## Phase 8 — Validation

1. `dotnet build` — zero errors.
2. `dotnet test` — all tests pass; counts match Phase 0 baseline.
3. Run a test exe directly: `./bin/Debug/net8.0/MyProject.Tests.exe` — confirms MTP is active.
4. Push to ADO — confirm TRX results and coverage appear on the pipeline summary.
5. Verify VS / VS Code Test Explorer discovers & runs tests (VS 17.14+).

---

## Checklist

- [ ] `Directory.Build.props` sets `EnableMSTestRunner`, `OutputType Exe`, `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`
- [ ] TRX extension package added (Option A) or MSTest.Sdk used (Option B)
- [ ] CodeCoverage extension package added (Option A) or MSTest.Sdk used (Option B)
- [ ] No `VSTest@3` tasks remain in pipeline YAML
- [ ] `DotNetCoreCLI@2` uses `--` separator before MTP args
- [ ] `PublishTestResults@2` and `PublishCodeCoverageResults@2` tasks added
- [ ] `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` and `MSTest.TestAdapter` removed (post-verification)
- [ ] Test counts match pre-migration baseline
- [ ] No `vstest.console.exe` invocations remain anywhere

---

## Risks & Rollback

| Risk | Mitigation |
|------|------------|
| TRX not generated | Ensure `--report-trx` is after `--` separator |
| Coverage % differs | Check `IncludeTestAssembly` setting (defaults to `false` in MTP) |
| Exit code 8 breaks CI | Add `--ignore-exit-code 8` |
| Rollback | Revert to `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` + old packages + `VSTest@3` |

---

## Future — .NET 10 SDK

When you upgrade to .NET 10 SDK, replace `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport` with native
MTP mode in `global.json` and drop the `--` separator:

```json
{
  "test": { "runner": "Microsoft.Testing.Platform" }
}
```

 Centralize MTP configuration in `Directory.Build.props`, replace VSTest@3 with DotNetCoreCLI@2, add MTP extension packages for TRX and coverage, and use the `--` separator (required on .NET 8).
 
 ---
 
 ## Todos
 
 ### 1. Establish baseline
 - Run `dotnet test` and record pass/fail counts for all 5 projects before any changes.
 
 ### 2. Create/update Directory.Build.props
 Add at the solution/repo root (condition on your test project naming convention):
 
 ```xml
 <Project>
   <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
     <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
     <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
     <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
   </PropertyGroup>
 </Project>
 ```
 
 > Adjust the `.EndsWith('.Tests')` condition to match your actual naming convention.
 
 ### 3. Add MTP extension NuGet packages
 Add to each test project (or centrally via Directory.Packages.props if using CPM):
 
 ```xml

 <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Testing.Extensions.CodeCoverage" Version="17.13.0" />
 ```
